steps.title: Identify the starting value
steps.content: At the start of the observation, there are $44{,}000$ bacteria. That is the initial value of the exponential model.

steps.title: Use the daily multiplier
steps.content: The number of bacteria doubles every day, so the multiplier for each day is $2$. After $t$ days, the factor is $2^t$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: The model must start at $44{,}000$ when $t=0$ and double to $88{,}000$ when $t=1$. $$y=44{,}000(2)^t$$ The answer is D.
